intro: This report flags the sections of your fiber-trench run that didn't clear every quality check, so you know exactly what to fix before the job can close out. Bring it to the site, work through the flagged sections one by one, and re-shoot or correct whatever each card asks for. Nothing in here requires office action — every item is something the crew can resolve in the field.
situation: Of the 20 sections reviewed, 5 passed clean, 5 came back with warnings worth watching, and 10 are hard fails that need action before sign-off. The story is almost entirely one thing: half the sections have no photos attached at all, which by itself accounts for all 10 red verdicts. Beyond that, five individual sections each carry their own separate problem — a 31-metre gap between shot points, a location that plots 1.7 km away from where it should be, a segment where warning tape wasn't visible during the tape-laying phase, one section where a photo was reused from somewhere else, and one where the only photos on file were pulled for privacy or irrelevance and nothing usable was left. Your immediate next step is to take the crew out and photograph every unshot section, then circle back to address those five one-off issues with the specific fixes called out on each card.
closing: The rest of this document is organised by FCP route: sections that share the same 'no photos' problem are collapsed into a single summary block per route to keep things easy to scan, while sections with a unique issue each get their own full card spelling out exactly what went wrong and what to do. Passing sections and the complete list of eight quality checks are in the appendix at the back.
